summary.title: Sample Payments Layered Architecture
summary.description:
This document describes the payments architecture using authored functional design and inferred runtime/code realization.
It is intended to help product, platform, security, and implementation engineers reason about one shared model.
Functional design is kept stable while realization details are inferred from infrastructure and source artifacts.

diagrams.title: Sample Payments Layered Architecture Threat Model

data.description: Starts checkout and confirms payment.
data.name: Customer

data.description: Operates GitOps and platform lifecycle.
data.name: Platform Operator

data.description: Reviews high-risk and declined-payment cases.
data.name: Support Agent

data.name: Authorization Decision

threats.description: Replayed callback message tries to overwrite or duplicate prior decision outcomes.
threats.mitigation: Nonce freshness and idempotency lock reduce replay acceptance probability.
threats.title: Replayed bank callback triggers duplicate authorization transition

data.name: Authorization Request

threats.description: Crafted payload shape attempts to force ambiguous authorization behavior and bypass policy checks.
threats.mitigation: Strict schema decoding and normalized request construction reduce spoofing surface.
threats.title: Checkout payload spoofing bypasses normalization checks

data.description: schemas/review-ticket.json
data.name: Manual Review Ticket

threats.description: A compromised support or workflow path modifies review ticket outcomes before authorization completes.
threats.mitigation: MFA-gated support actions are in place; append-only integrity controls are being expanded.
threats.title: Manual review ticket tampering influences authorization result

data.description: schemas/risk-signal.json
data.name: Risk Signal

data.description:
Checkout handling is the user-facing entrypoint where payment requests are initiated and normalized.
It validates request shape, preserves transaction context, and returns clear customer feedback for each outcome.
It delegates decision logic to payment authorization while protecting the user experience boundary.
data.name: Checkout Handling

threats.description: Crafted payload shape attempts to force ambiguous authorization behavior and bypass policy checks.
threats.mitigation: Strict schema decoding and normalized request construction reduce spoofing surface.
threats.title: Checkout payload spoofing bypasses normalization checks

data.description:
Cluster provisioning creates the runtime substrate used by all application workloads.
It establishes cluster and namespace structure, baseline controls, and environment-level readiness.
This unit is responsible for predictable, repeatable infrastructure foundations.
data.name: Cluster Provisioning

data.description:
GitOps operations continuously reconciles intended release state with actual runtime state.
It governs rollout flow, drift correction, and operational delivery confidence across payment and fraud workloads.
The unit provides safe and observable release behavior as an ongoing operational capability.
data.name: GitOps Operations

data.description:
Payment authorization orchestrates the final transaction decision path.
It coordinates fraud scoring, external bank interactions, and escalation to support review when needed.
The unit returns deterministic outcomes so downstream behavior remains consistent and testable.
data.name: Payment Authorization

threats.description: Replayed callback message tries to overwrite or duplicate prior decision outcomes.
threats.mitigation: Nonce freshness and idempotency lock reduce replay acceptance probability.
threats.title: Replayed bank callback triggers duplicate authorization transition

threats.description: A compromised support or workflow path modifies review ticket outcomes before authorization completes.
threats.mitigation: MFA-gated support actions are in place; append-only integrity controls are being expanded.
threats.title: Manual review ticket tampering influences authorization result

data.description:
Risk scoring computes and classifies transaction risk before approval decisions are finalized.
It provides a stable scoring contract to authorization and supports audit context for later analysis.
The unit is focused on decision quality, consistency, and policy-driven classification behavior.
data.name: Risk Scoring

data.description:
Support review handles manual decisions for escalated or ambiguous payment cases.
It gives support operators context to approve, reject, or request additional verification in a controlled flow.
The unit ensures manual intervention remains auditable, policy-constrained, and operationally reliable.
data.name: Support Review

threats.description: A compromised support or workflow path modifies review ticket outcomes before authorization completes.
threats.mitigation: MFA-gated support actions are in place; append-only integrity controls are being expanded.
threats.title: Manual review ticket tampering influences authorization result
